title: rinorea racemosa
intro: Rinorea racemosa, also known as the "Burmese poison," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Violaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Rinorea racemosa include its potential to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent. Rinorea racemosa can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia.
